# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require 'json'
require 'base64'

class SafeSessionStore
  ALLOWED_TYPES = [String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Array, Hash, Symbol].freeze
  MAX_SESSION_SIZE = 4096 # Cookie size limit
  SESSION_COOKIE_KEY = :session

  def initialize(app, options = {})
    @app = app
    @options = {
      key: SESSION_COOKIE_KEY,
      expire_after: options.fetch(:expire_after, 3600),
      secure: options.fetch(:secure, true),
      httponly: options.fetch(:httponly, true),
      same_site: options.fetch(:same_site, :lax)
    }
  end

  def call(env)
    request = Rack::Request.new(env)
    session_data = deserialize(request.cookies[@options[:key].to_s])
    env['rack.session'] = session_data

    status, headers, body = @app.call(env)

    response = Rack::Response.new(body, status, headers)
    save_session(response, env['rack.session'])

    response.finish
  end

  # Serialize session data using JSON (safe data-only format) and Base64 encoding
  # NOTE: We intentionally use JSON instead of Marshal for serialization.
  # Marshal.load on untrusted data (such as cookies) is vulnerable to
  # remote code execution attacks. An attacker can craft a malicious
  # serialized payload that executes arbitrary code when deserialized.
  # JSON is a safe data-only format that cannot encode executable objects.
  def serialize(data)
    return nil if data.nil? || data.empty?

    validated_data = deep_validate_and_sanitize(data)
    json_string = JSON.generate(validated_data)
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(json_string)

    if encoded.bytesize > MAX_SESSION_SIZE
      raise SessionSizeExceeded, "Serialized session data exceeds maximum cookie size of #{MAX_SESSION_SIZE} bytes"
    end

    encoded
  end

  # Deserialize session data using JSON (safe data-only format)
  # We use JSON.parse instead of Marshal.load to prevent deserialization attacks.
  def deserialize(encoded_data)
    return {} if encoded_data.nil? || encoded_data.empty?

    decoded_bytes = Base64.strict_decode64(encoded_data)
    parsed_data = JSON.parse(decoded_bytes)

    unless parsed_data.is_a?(Hash)
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Session data must deserialize to a Hash, got #{parsed_data.class}"
    end

    validated = deep_validate_and_sanitize(parsed_data)
    validated
  rescue Base64::DecodeError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SafeSessionStore: Invalid Base64 in session cookie: #{e.message}") if defined?(Rails) && Rails.respond_to?(:logger)
    {}
  rescue JSON::ParserError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SafeSessionStore: Invalid JSON in session cookie: #{e.message}") if defined?(Rails) && Rails.respond_to?(:logger)
    {}
  rescue InvalidSessionData => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SafeSessionStore: #{e.message}") if defined?(Rails) && Rails.respond_to?(:logger)
    {}
  rescue StandardError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SafeSessionStore: Unexpected error deserializing session: #{e.message}") if defined?(Rails) && Rails.respond_to?(:logger)
    {}
  end

  private

  def save_session(response, session_data)
    serialized = serialize(session_data || {})

    if serialized.nil?
      response.delete_cookie(@options[:key].to_s)
    else
      cookie_options = {
        value: serialized,
        path: '/',
        httponly: @options[:httponly],
        secure: @options[:secure],
        same_site: @options[:same_site]
      }
      cookie_options[:expires] = Time.now + @options[:expire_after] if @options[:expire_after]
      response.set_cookie(@options[:key].to_s, cookie_options)
    end
  rescue SessionSizeExceeded => e
    Rails.logger.error("SafeSessionStore: #{e.message}") if defined?(Rails) && Rails.respond_to?(:logger)
  end

  # Recursively validate that all values in the data structure are safe types.
  # This ensures no unexpected object types slip through after deserialization.
  def deep_validate_and_sanitize(obj, depth = 0)
    raise InvalidSessionData, "Session data nesting too deep (max 10 levels)" if depth > 10

    case obj
    when Hash
      sanitized = {}
      obj.each do |key, value|
        sanitized_key = validate_hash_key(key)
        sanitized[sanitized_key] = deep_validate_and_sanitize(value, depth + 1)
      end
      sanitized
    when Array
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Session array too large (max 1000 elements)" if obj.length > 1000
      obj.map { |element| deep_validate_and_sanitize(element, depth + 1) }
    when String
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Session string value too large (max 10KB)" if obj.bytesize > 10_240
      obj.dup.freeze
    when Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      obj
    when Symbol
      # Convert symbols to strings for safe JSON round-tripping
      obj.to_s
    else
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Disallowed type in session data: #{obj.class}. " \
        "Only #{ALLOWED_TYPES.map(&:name).join(', ')} are permitted."
    end
  end

  def validate_hash_key(key)
    case key
    when String
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Session hash key too large (max 256 bytes)" if key.bytesize > 256
      key.dup.freeze
    when Symbol
      key.to_s
    when Integer
      key.to_s
    else
      raise InvalidSessionData, "Disallowed hash key type: #{key.class}. Keys must be String, Symbol, or Integer."
    end
  end

  class SessionSizeExceeded < StandardError; end
  class InvalidSessionData < StandardError; end
end

# Rails-compatible session store that integrates with ActionDispatch
if defined?(ActionDispatch)
  class SafeRailsSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
    ALLOWED_TYPES = SafeSessionStore::ALLOWED_TYPES

    def initialize(app, options = {})
      super
      @safe_store = SafeSessionStore.new(app, options)
    end

    private

    def find_session(req, session_id)
      session_id ||= generate_sid
      data = @safe_store.deserialize(req.cookie_jar[session_cookie_name])
      [session_id, data]
    end

    def write_session(req, session_id, session_data, _options)
      serialized = @safe_store.serialize(session_data)
      req.cookie_jar[session_cookie_name] = {
        value: serialized,
        httponly: true,
        secure: req.ssl?,
        same_site: :lax
      }
      session_id
    rescue SafeSessionStore::SessionSizeExceeded, SafeSessionStore::InvalidSessionData => e
      Rails.logger.error("SafeRailsSessionStore: #{e.message}") if Rails.logger
      false
    end

    def delete_session(req, session_id, _options)
      req.cookie_jar.delete(session_cookie_name)
      generate_sid
    end

    def session_cookie_name
      @options.fetch(:key, '_session')
    end
  end
end
